abstract.para: You can create clone resources using either &hawk; or &crmsh;.

listitem.para: You can perform this procedure on any node in the cluster.

step.para: Log in either as the &rootuser; user or as a user with sudo privileges.

step.screen: &prompt.user;sudo crm configure primitive Apache apache \ op monitor timeout=20s interval=10s

listitem.para: You can access the &hawk; Web interface from any machine that has network access to the nodes.

step.para: In a Web browser, go to https://HAWKSERVER:7630/ and log in.
From the left navigation bar, select Configuration Add Resource Clone .
From the Child Resource list, select the primitive or group to use as a subresource for the clone.
If needed, modify or add Meta Attributes.
Click Create to finish the configuration. A message at the top of the screen shows if the action has been successful.

topic.para: You can create promotable clones using either &hawk; or &crmsh;.

listitem.para: You can perform this procedure on any node in the cluster.

step.para: Log in either as the &rootuser; user or as a user with sudo privileges.
Configure the primitive. Change the intervals if needed:
step.screen: &prompt.user;sudo crm configure primitive my-rsc ocf:myCorp:myAppl \ op monitor interval=60 \ op monitor interval=61 role=Promoted
&prompt.user;sudo crm configure clone clone-rsc my-rsc meta promotable=true

listitem.para: You can access the &hawk; Web interface from any machine that has network access to the nodes.

step.para: In a Web browser, go to https://HAWKSERVER:7630/ and log in.
From the left navigation bar, select Configuration Add Resource Multi-state .
From the Child Resource list, select the primitive or group to use as a subresource for the multi-state resource.
If needed, modify or add Meta Attributes.
Click Create to finish the configuration. A message at the top of the screen shows if the action has been successful.
